title: Isolated Docker Environments for Parallel Claude Code Worktrees

requirements.title: No plaintext secrets in generated override file
requirements.description: The generated docker-compose.worktree.yml MUST NOT contain plaintext secret values (API keys, JWT secrets, encryption keys, OAuth credentials). Use env_file directives or ${VAR} interpolation for all sensitive configuration.

validation.description: Grep the generated override file for hardcoded secret patterns. Result must be zero matches.

requirements.implementation_notes: Only set non-secret overrides as literal values: port mappings, CORS_ORIGINS (localhost URLs), FRONTEND_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL, COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME, DATABASE_URL (using the same well-known dev credentials from the base compose file). Secrets from .env files should flow through the existing env_file mechanism.

requirements.title: Strict issue number validation before any shell use
requirements.description: The issue number argument MUST be validated as a positive integer in range 1-62535 before any use in shell commands, file paths, Docker commands, or string interpolation.

validation.description: Run the script with each test input and verify correct accept/reject behavior.

requirements.implementation_notes: Use regex validation: [[ "$ISSUE_NUM" =~ ^[1-9][0-9]*$ ]] followed by arithmetic check: (( ISSUE_NUM >= 1 && ISSUE_NUM <= 62535 )). Must appear BEFORE any variable interpolation.

requirements.title: CORS origins must be explicit localhost, never wildcard
requirements.description: The CORS_ORIGINS value in the generated override file MUST be a JSON array containing only the specific http://localhost:<port> origin for the worktree's frontend. Never use '*' or include origins beyond what is needed.

validation.command: python3 -c "import json, sys; v=json.loads(sys.argv[1]); assert isinstance(v, list); assert all(o.startswith('http://localhost:') for o in v); assert '*' not in v"
validation.description: Parse the CORS_ORIGINS value from the generated file and verify it is a JSON array of explicit localhost URLs with no wildcards.

requirements.implementation_notes: Template: CORS_ORIGINS: '["http://localhost:<3000+N>"]'. The pydantic-settings parser for cors_origins: list[str] will reject malformed JSON, causing a safe startup failure.

requirements.title: Bind ports to 127.0.0.1 only in generated override
requirements.description: The generated override file SHOULD use '127.0.0.1:<host-port>:<container-port>' format for all port mappings to prevent exposure to the local network.

validation.description: All port mappings in the override file must start with 127.0.0.1. No bare port mappings like '8000:8000' should appear.

requirements.implementation_notes: Use format: '127.0.0.1:${FRONTEND_PORT}:3000', '127.0.0.1:${BACKEND_PORT}:8000', '127.0.0.1:${DB_PORT}:5432'. This overrides the base compose file's less-restrictive bindings.

requirements.title: Unique COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME per worktree
requirements.description: Each worktree Docker stack MUST use a unique COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME (e.g., openlearning-wt-<N>) to ensure container, network, and volume isolation from other worktrees and the main repo stack.

validation.description: Start two worktree stacks and verify docker ps shows containers with different project name prefixes. Verify docker network ls shows separate networks.

requirements.implementation_notes: Use -p flag or COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME environment variable. The project name must contain only [a-z0-9-] characters. Derived as: openlearning-wt-${ISSUE_NUM}.

requirements.title: Volume mount paths use only validated directory paths
requirements.description: All volume mount paths in the generated override YAML MUST be constructed from the validated worktree directory path. No unsanitized user input may appear in mount specifications.

validation.description: Trace all volume mount values in the generation template back to variables derived from the validated issue number and known directory constants.

requirements.implementation_notes: WORKTREE_DIR is constructed as WORKTREE_BASE/issue-$ISSUE_NUM where ISSUE_NUM is validated as a positive integer. No additional user input should flow into volume paths.

requirements.title: Override file regenerated on every invocation
requirements.description: The script MUST overwrite any existing override file unconditionally on each run. Use truncation (>) not append (>>). Combine with --force-recreate -V to prevent Docker from caching stale state.

validation.description: Modify the override file manually, run make worktree-dev, verify the modification is overwritten.

requirements.implementation_notes: Use shell redirection > to write the file. The Docker --force-recreate -V flags handle container and anonymous volume recreation.

requirements.title: Document Docker privilege implications
requirements.description: The worktree dev documentation SHOULD note that Docker-based development requires Docker Desktop and that Docker containers have broad host access capabilities.

validation.description: Review documentation for Docker privilege note.

requirements.implementation_notes: Add a brief note in the worktree dev docs. No code change needed.

requirements.title: Non-racy symlink removal
requirements.description: Symlink detection and removal SHOULD use unconditional operations (rm -f for files, rm -rf for directories) rather than check-then-act patterns to avoid TOCTOU races.

validation.description: Verify the script does not have a gap between checking if a symlink exists and removing it.

requirements.implementation_notes: Pattern: [ -L frontend/node_modules ] && rm frontend/node_modules || true; npm install. The || true ensures the script continues if node_modules is not a symlink.

requirements.title: Quote ISSUE variable in Makefile targets
requirements.description: Makefile targets SHOULD pass the $(ISSUE) variable in double quotes to prevent word splitting before bash receives the argument.

validation.description: Verify all Makefile worktree targets use quoted variable: @bash scripts/worktree-dev.sh "$(ISSUE)".

requirements.implementation_notes: Change all worktree-related Makefile targets to quote the variable. Also apply to existing worktree-create and worktree-remove targets for consistency.

requirements.title: Override file within gitignored directory
requirements.description: The generated override file MUST be placed within the .claude/worktrees/issue-<N>/ directory, which is already covered by .gitignore, to prevent accidental commit.

validation.description: After generating the override file, git status must not show it as untracked or staged.

requirements.implementation_notes: Place file at .claude/worktrees/issue-<N>/docker-compose.worktree.yml. Use absolute paths with -f flags when invoking docker compose.
